Petition For Undue Influence And For Recovery Of Beneficial Interest And Injunctive Relief
Off Calendar May-21-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Stecher for Sarah Del Monte and Kenneth Del Monte (Respondents). Gregory Moll (Petitioner) in propria persona.

All parties stipulated to the Commissioner sitting as a Judge Pro Temp. Mr. Stecher stated that he was satisfied with the tentative ruling and he did not receive notice of intent to argue. Mr. Moll stated that he was not aware of the tentative ruling and that he had not informed opposing counsel of his intent to argue. The Court adopted the tentative ruling:

General and special demurrer based on each of the five (5) grounds raised:

1. General demurrer to 1st cause of action; Tentative ruling: Sustain with 10 days leave to amend based on failure to state a cause of action.

2. General demurrer against Trust; Tenative ruling: Sustain with 10 days leave to amend based on failure to state a cause of action.

3. Special demurrer; Tentative ruling: Sustain with 10 days leave to amend based on uncertainty.

4. General demurrer; Tentative ruling: Sustain with 10 days leave to amend based on standing.

5. General demurrer to causes of action 2 - 4; Tentative ruling: Sustain with 10 days leave to amend based on failure to state a cause of action.

The motion to strike was taken off calendar. Order for the same signed in open court.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley, CSR#. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.
